mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, driving immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns as investors reprice assets to higher present values.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns do not exceed 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when the unemployment rate rises by over 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand growth, which boosts corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a positive rank IC over 60-day horizons, or forward returns are negative in over 50% of co-occurring expansion months.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side shocks that disrupt manufacturing output and supply chains, triggering rapid negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines do not correlate with negative 5-day forward returns, or the signal loses predictive power during recovery phases with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation readings that breach the 2.5% annualized threshold force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of months when CPI exceeds the 2.5% annualized threshold, invalidating the tightening fear mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined payroll and unemployment signal yields a negative rank IC over 60-day horizons, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of aligned months.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real GDP growth directly expand corporate revenue baselines and profit margins, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth signals fail to predict 20-day forward returns with a rank IC above 0.01, or positive returns drop below 50% during expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index drops exceeding 1.0% month-over-month indicate severe manufacturing contractions that immediately trigger risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in over 60% of instances when industrial production falls by more than 1.0%, negating the supply shock signal.
